message:
So last month I burned dinner—like, *really* burned it—and our smoke alarm went off while I was frantically opening windows. My first instinct? Grab my phone and text the family group chat "DON'T PANIC, IT'S JUST ME." 😅 My teenager replied "MOM YOU SCARED ME" and my partner was already heading home. That moment made me realize we'd never actually talked about what a real fire alarm means in our house. Like, do the kids know to get out first and meet us outside? Where? We literally just... assumed everyone would figure it out.

It took maybe 15 minutes to walk through an actual escape plan together after that. Nothing scary—just "if you smell smoke, don't hide, get low, get out this way, meet at the mailbox." We even tested the smoke alarms (they actually worked, phew). Now when the alarm goes off, there's no confusion.

Feels way better than crossing my fingers and hoping everyone magically knows what to do.

Does your family have a fire escape plan, or is that still on the to-do list?
